inputs.prompt: Programme: 'The News Hour' — evening current affairs, serious and fair. Guest: Sarah Chen, housing policy researcher at a university think tank. Topic: New government figures showing the average first-time buyer deposit has reached an all-time high. Purpose: Explanation and mild accountability. Duration: 5 minutes. Live.
Guest: A local bookshop owner. Topic: Independent bookshops thriving despite online competition. Purpose: Human interest. Duration: 4 minutes.

Programme: 'Studio Debat' — Dutch current affairs radio, direct and probing. Guest: Dr. Karel Verhoeven, former advisor to the European Commission on digital regulation. Topic: The EU AI Act — now six months since implementation, first enforcement cases emerging. Purpose: Accountability and explanation. Duration: 8 minutes. Sensitive: Do not ask about his departure from the Commission. Background: Enforcement has been slow; industry groups claim compliance costs are destroying innovation.

Programme: 'Hard Talk' style — extended one-on-one with an uncomfortable guest. Guest: The CEO of a social media company whose platform was named in a parliamentary report as contributing to youth mental health harm. The CEO has agreed to the interview but has a PR team feeding answers through an earpiece. Topic: Platform responsibility for youth mental health. Purpose: Accountability. Duration: 15 minutes. Live. Background: The company's own internal research (leaked) showed they knew about harm to teenage users. The CEO will attempt to pivot to 'parental responsibility' and 'we've invested $100 million in safety' talking points. The cue sheet should anticipate and counter these deflections.

Programme: 'India Today' — flagship current affairs radio, authoritative and direct. Guest: A retired Supreme Court justice who has publicly criticised the government's use of sedition laws against journalists. Topic: Press freedom and judicial independence. Purpose: Analysis and accountability. Duration: 10 minutes. Pre-recorded. Sensitive: The guest is elderly and respected — the tone should be deferential but the questions must be pointed. Do not ask about specific pending cases. Background: Three journalists have been arrested this month under laws the guest has called 'colonial relics.'

Programme: 'Morning Show' — breakfast radio, upbeat and fun. Guest: A comedian promoting their new stand-up special. Topic: The special, their creative process, and a recent viral moment where they roasted a heckler. Purpose: Entertainment, promotion. Duration: 6 minutes. Live. Background: The comedian is sharp, fast, and will try to take over the interview. The cue sheet should include moments where the presenter can regain control and steer toward the plug. The comedian's publicist has asked that family topics are off-limits.
